zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda (Maud) de Vitre於大約1146年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉維特雷時，她的父親Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars28歲，母親Emma de Dinan18歲。她於大約1164年與Henry de la Pomeraie在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1183年在英國英格蘭戴文享年38歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vitre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Argentina): adaptation of a surname such as Greek   Mitropoulos   and Italian   Mitri  .
Albanian and Romanian: from the personal name Mitre, a short form of Albanian Dhimitër, Romanian Dumitru, from classical Greek Dēmētrios (see Dimitris ).

variants.name: Mitropoulos
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Mitros + the ending -poulos (from Latin pullus ‘nestling, chick’), which occurs chiefly in the Peloponnese. Compare Metropoulos .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: (Maud)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda (Maud) de Vitre was born about 1146, in Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, her father, Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars, was 28 and her mother, Emma de Dinan, was 18. She married Henry de la Pomeraie about 1164, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1183, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda (Maud) de Vitre est née environ 1146, à Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Emma de Dinan, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Henry de la Pomeraie environ 1164, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1183, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda (Maud) de Vitreが約1146年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインヴィトルeで生まれた時，父Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillarsは28歳，母Emma de Dinanは18歳であった。約1164年にイギリスイギリスデボンでHenry de la Pomeraieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1183年にイギリスイギリスデボンで38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda (Maud) de Vitre(이)가 대략 1146년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 비트e에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Emma de Dinan(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1164년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Henry de la Pomeraie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1183년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda (Maud) de Vitre nasceu aproximadamente 1146, em Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Emma de Dinan, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry de la Pomeraie aproximadamente 1164, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda (Maud) de Vitre родилась около 1146 г. в местности витрe, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Robert de Vitré, III, Lord of Tillars было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma de Dinan, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de la Pomeraie около 1164 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1183 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет.
